title: Why was Shinji upset that Misato was having with Kaji
display_title: Why was Shinji upset that Misato was having with Kaji
wikitext:
[]: Why was Shinji upset that Misato was having with Kaji             Quote Post by e » Mon Apr 18, 2011 3:31 pm why was Shinji upset that Misato was having with Kaji if the fool himself was ing to Asuka in her hospital bed. kind of hypocritical if you ask me

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Apr 18, 2011 3:39 pm http://wiki.evageeks.org/EvaGeeks_Forum_Rules_and_Guidelines#Posting Make sure that your posts are coherent and understandable. Others should not be required to perform “translation” when reading.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Mon Apr 18, 2011 3:41 pm "Women must be PURE and UNTARNISHED in order to be good people, wanting & enjoying sex is dirrrty." - attitude. And general fear of intimacy (which might actually be behind that attitude too). I have let myself believe that that attitude is actually quite prevalent in many places, including Japan. I personally do think that that attitude is stupid but then again I'm a liberal leftist hippie. And it's not like he held himself in high regard after the hospital room thing either.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Apr 18, 2011 3:47 pm It's like thinking about your parents having sex, even if Misato is only a sort of foster-mother.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Mon Apr 18, 2011 4:09 pm Sexual jealousy. He wanted to be first.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Apr 18, 2011 4:41 pm Allemann wrote: Sexual jealousy. He wanted to be first.

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Mon Apr 18, 2011 8:27 pm Azathoth wrote: A combination of these plus the fact that he's a huge prude in general.

[]:             Quote Post by LordEvan88 » Mon Apr 18, 2011 9:01 pm He's sexually repressed. Its kind of a step beyond prudishness but it ties into his general intimacy issues. He probably wants sex just like any other 14 year old boy, but he is so afraid of other people he can't cope with that. Beyond that its just really weird to see anyone you know on a non-sexual level having sex. Its kind of weird seeing anyone honestly having sex (I am not counting porn, where generally the entire act is so over-dramatized it looks nothing like sex). Its kind of the removal of what we typically identify as human behavior, its a thoroughly primal act of seeking pleasure that is quite...undignified. When you develop a certain bond with people of any gender it kind of becomes weird thinking of them sexually, because its so far removed from regular interaction. So Shinji does kind of feel it weird seeing two people whom he looks up to and who were his friends/mentors going at it. I mean, I can't imagine either of them look bad with their clothes off (This is an anime), but its a side of a person they keep separate from the rest of themselves. Also, as a sidenote, you say, think, and do really weird things in the throes of passion, things that kind of don't leave that place because they don't belong anywhere else.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Apr 18, 2011 11:04 pm During the Misato/Kaji having sex flashback during EoE, Shinji says "This is Misato? She does... this?"

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Apr 19, 2011 3:18 am I don't know if Shinji was really upset or just disillusioned. We build up our perceptions of the people in our lives and to see them outside that can be a shocking experience, even when it's something we "know" they do or someone we "know" they are. Call it a kind of willful ignorance that we don't like shattered.

[]: Re: Why was Shinji upset that Misato was having with Kaji             Quote Post by Leighton » Tue Apr 19, 2011 4:15 am e wrote: why was Shinji upset that Misato was having with Kaji if the fool himself was ing to Asuka in her hospital bed. kind of hypocritical if you ask me

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Tue Apr 19, 2011 5:18 am It's a little narrow just to say that he was "upset." It's probably a combination of a lot of things. It's actually struck me how very nuanced his expression is while he's witnessing the sex scene. You can read a hell of a lot from it. To me, he looks confused and uncertain above all else. Also something to consider: it might just be the first time he's actually seen sex. Considering his general attitude, I doubt he has much experience with porn, so it's probably a bit of a shocker.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Apr 19, 2011 6:28 am BornIn1142 wrote: It's a little narrow just to say that he was "upset." It's probably a combination of a lot of things. It's actually struck me how very nuanced his expression is while he's witnessing the sex scene. You can read a hell of a lot from it. To me, he looks confused and uncertain above all else. Also something to consider: it might just be the first time he's actually seen sex. Considering his general attitude, I doubt he has much experience with porn, so it's probably a bit of a shocker.

[]:             Quote Post by LordEvan88 » Tue Apr 19, 2011 11:17 am BornIn1142 wrote: Also something to consider: it might just be the first time he's actually seen sex. Considering his general attitude, I doubt he has much experience with porn, so it's probably a bit of a shocker.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Wed Apr 20, 2011 5:33 pm I think "upset" is a bit too broad a term to apply to this scene, but then again it's so emotionally complex that few words could adequately capture it. I can't really describe the feelings this scene invokes in ordinary words, but the best I can do is try and encourage you to imagine it for yourself. Look at it this way: Shinji has this very intricate and strange relationship with Misato, where she's kind of a mother figure, sister figure, love interest and object of sexual desire all at the same time, and Shinji himself has some pretty deepseated issues with his own sexuality as we all know. Now, given all of those things combined, try and imagine how he must have felt seeing this woman who he looks up to engaging in this very adult act - an act that involves shedding one's inhibitions and persona, and being truly "naked" in more than just a literal sense - that he himself is uncertain and afraid of. It's troubling and explosive, for certain, and Shinji, being Shinji, most likely has no idea what to feel about it. Maybe some of this is just my interpretation of the character, but then, this is Eva - some degree of personal inference is only to be expected.

[]: Re: Why was Shinji upset that Misato was having with Kaji             Quote Post by violaine » Thu Apr 21, 2011 12:21 am Leighton wrote: Shinji wanked to Asuka as a means to confirm his mundane existence.

[]: Re: Why was Shinji upset that Misato was having with Kaji             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Apr 21, 2011 12:22 am violaine wrote: i sincerely miss the times when people masturbated for the sake of masturbation.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Apr 21, 2011 12:28 am It was his first glimpse of adult female bush. The look on his face was of perplexed horror.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Thu Apr 21, 2011 2:17 am That scene and Shinji's reaction reminds me a little bit of the one from episode four, when he's in the mostly empty theater sitting through the 'second impact documentary' and happens to notice a couple makin' out. That was probably one of the first moments from the tv series where I felt like I kind of related to Shinji. I can still clearly recall, with alarmingly little effort, what it was like to be about fourteen years old, having recently moved into a new town and enrolled in a new school, with almost zero experience even talking to girls (never mind doing anything else), a jerk for for a father and feeling basically depressed as hell regarding all of the above. Right around that time I was even suspended from school for a few days as punishment for standing up to a teacher and refusing after he'd ordered me to pick up someone else's trash off the floor. Granted, it's not quite the same thing as Shinji's disagreement with Misato over how to deal with Shamshel, but I think the whole "distrust of authority thing when you know you're right/they're wrong" definately still applies. My point is that I think there are moments in a young man's life when you just feel rotten, like you don't have a friend in the world and that the situation is unlikely to improve any time soon. If you're someone like Shinji, maybe you deal with it by hanging out in an empty theater; perhaps you can hope to sort things out during Instrumentality (or, as in the case of EoTV, some combination of the two). Either way, what I think we can all agree upon is that when the going gets rough and you're hip deep in depression, the last thing you really need to see is two people getting their freak on right in front of you. I can personally attest that this is seldom helpful and, in most cases, only serves to accentuate how dysfunctional one tends to feel in comparison. Not to disagree with what others have posted here, but I don't think it's entirely fair to label an inexperienced fourteen year old kid as either a prude or sexually repressed just because he would prefer not to have a front row seat to other people's intimate moments. Then again, what the hell do I know? Turns out I'm a weird guy who thinks that it probably wouldn't kill most of the moderately to extremely successful mating pairs out there to dial back their behavior a bit and stop flaunting their sexual prowess around the non-significant-other-having Shinjis of the world. Maybe they could show a little class and get a room, or something.

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sat Apr 23, 2011 12:44 am One word: Cougar! Ever kid his age wants a cougar to date.

 and happens to notice a couple makin' out. That was probably one of the first moments from the tv series where I felt like I kind of related to Shinji. I can still clearly recall, with alarmingly little effort, what it was like to be about fourteen years old, having recently moved into a new town and enrolled in a new school, with almost zero experience even talking to girls (never mind doing anything else), a jerk for for a father and feeling basically depressed as hell regarding all of the above. If you're someone like Shinji, maybe you deal with it by hanging out in an empty theater; perhaps you can hope to sort things out during Instrumentality (or, as in the case of EoTV, some combination of the two). Either way, what I think we can all agree upon is that when the going gets rough and you're hip deep in depression, the last thing you really need to see is two people getting their freak on right in front of you. I can personally attest that this is seldom helpful and, in most cases, only serves to accentuate how dysfunctional one tends to feel in comparison. Then again, what the hell do I know?
